approach: additive-only on the frozen suite: the capture scenarios are new, no existing scenario narrows or is rewritten, so the freeze self-clears and no re-open ratification is needed. The 'list, show and validate answer with no multiplexer at all' outline stays true verbatim — save is simply not among its Examples.

the node's README is prose and never frozen, so reversing the recorded 'layout export' non-goal is a plain revise. It also corrects the 'managing templates never touches a multiplexer' claim, which save is the first exception to.

seam addition: a new portable describeRegion verb on SessionAdapter. Per the house style this node already set (Gap A's `from`, then ratio/env), a seam addition driven by layout is specified through layout.feature rather than folded back into mux. Open at intake: whether the member is required or optional — required is a shape change to an exported interface for any external implementor, which is cheap on a 0.x package but is a real decision, not a default.

the implementation exists as a pre-spec spike (shot-before-aim) proven against live tmux 3.6b and herdr 0.7.4. It is carried in as learning, not as done: its tests bind to nothing under the verify-scenarios bridge, and the spec gate may reshape it.

user ratified two things at intake, both overriding the design of record: the verb is `layout save` (not `layout export`) and it writes a file rather than printing to stdout. Design §6.4's honesty argument for stdout is superseded by carrying the draft note in the file's own description, because layout list shows a saved capture beside finished templates.

why: cold sdd-spec-judge (independent subagent, fresh context) judged the revised layout/ node — README.md use cases + 22 scenarios added to the frozen layout.feature — backward against {oracle,builder,architect}: all PASS, ALIGNED true, zero failing scenarios, no blocker, no open markers. It re-ran the edit classification itself (gherkin-cli diff: 22 added / 0 modified / 0 removed / 46 unchanged, addOnly true) rather than trusting the brief, confirming the freeze self-clears with no re-open. It ran the miss test over every new scenario and found none inert, and ran the absorption check in the direction that actually threatened this CR: the scenarios were authored AFTER an implementation spike, so it checked whether the spike's live-captured cell counts leaked into any Given, and confirmed the Givens state preconditions while the numbers stay in trailing comments. It independently verified that the pre-existing 'list, show and validate answer with no multiplexer at all' outline stays true (save reads a mux but is not among its Examples), and that open issue #10 — which tracks the house style this CR follows for specifying a seam addition through layout.feature — is real and correctly deferred rather than absorbed. One cosmetic content gap (a missing behavior-table row for the rects-not-a-tree invariant) was fixed before this write. Two impl-side observations carried to deliver: describeRegion is declared required in session.ts but the ratified decision and the .feature both specify it optional, and the spike's docstrings still say 'layout export' and reference a layout-export.ts that no longer exists. Mechanical check-suite + check-spec-state green. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by agent) — ratify or kick back.

FOUR cold sdd-impl-judge rounds, each a fresh context re-deriving every frozen scenario's oracle independently. Three returned a change verdict on real defects — every one found by MUTATION rather than by reading, which is the finding about this CR's process worth carrying forward. R1: captureLayout had no duplicate-label check, so a region with two panes sharing a title produced a template with two identical labels, which the schema rejects because a label is the manifest's key — save silently wrote a file its own validate rejected. Live-reproduced against real tmux; fixed by dropping a colliding label from every pane carrying it and warning, with one additive scenario appended to specify the choice. R2: three scenarios were bound to tests that never exercised the CLI-observable half of their Then, proven by disabling stderr forwarding in cli.ts and watching all 97 tests stay green; fixed by moving each bound test to CLI level. R3: two over-claiming checks, the subtler being that this CR's own duplicate-label fix MASKED the tmux hostname filter — a fixture with two host-titled panes would collide and be dropped, producing the correct output for entirely the wrong reason, so a regression in the filter would have shipped green. R4 mutation-tested the eight behaviors no prior round had probed and found every one caught, passing with no blocker. One further defect was self-caught and reported rather than quietly fixed: the test bound to 'a captured template passes validate' asserted only that the name and description came back and never ran the validator — the very test that should have caught R1's bug. All 23 new scenarios bind and pass under the verify-scenarios bridge (69/28/28/0/41); the 41 unbound are the pre-existing baseline, measured at 46/5/41 on clean HEAD, and are being filed as a follow-up rather than absorbed. pnpm verify green, 415 tests. Rebased onto the target tip before the gate, so the judges saw the tree that lands. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by agent) — ratify or kick back.

title: Bind the layout node's pre-existing scenarios: 41 of 69 are UNBOUND because their tests sit under the wrong spec node
note: The verify-scenarios bridge is wired for this project and reports 69 total / 28 bound / 41 unbound for cyber-mux/layout. The 41 predate this CR — measured at 46 scenarios / 5 bound / 41 unbound on clean HEAD before any of this CR's work. The cause is structural rather than per-test: the CLI test file's only top-level describe is the mux node's segment, so every layout scenario verified at CLI level binds to the wrong node and therefore to nothing. This CR bound all 23 of its own scenarios by opening a second top-level describe on the layout node's segment; the same move would bind most of the 41. Not absorbed into this CR because it is orthogonal to capturing a region and touches tests for behavior this CR does not change. Consequence while it stands: the impl-judge hand-judges those 41 every round, which is the exact token burn the bridge exists to remove.

title: The round-trip check models a backend that draws no divider, so it cannot catch a divider-specific regression
note: The scenario asserting that re-applying a capture reproduces its region is verified against a hand-written layout simulator modelling herdr semantics, where panes tile the region exactly. On a backend that draws a divider between panes (tmux) the arithmetic differs, and the impl-judge confirmed by mutation that the naive ratio formula and a coarser rounding both survive this check — they are caught only by the separate dedicated tests. The round trip was verified live end-to-end on both backends during the mission, but that proof is not in the suite. A divider-aware model, or a check driven through the real walk rather than a test-local simulator, would close the gap. Raised by the round-1 impl-judge as explicitly non-blocking.

title: Two near-duplicate label-uniqueness loops, at capture time and at validate time
note: The capture drops a duplicated label and warns; the validator hard-rejects one. Both are short set-membership loops over different input shapes (a live pane list versus a parsed tree) serving deliberately different purposes, and the capture module documents why they must differ — so this is not duplication to collapse today. Worth revisiting only if a third label-uniqueness site appears. Raised by the round-4 impl-judge as explicitly non-blocking.
